source: Official GGG patch notes and official forum behavior traces

Definition.text: Corpse is a corpse object on the ground that can be consumed by corpse-dependent skills and systems.

Purpose.text: Provide fuel for corpse skills and effects that require corpse interactions.
Purpose.source: official patch notes references to corpse collection/itemization

Core Rules.text: A skill that requires a corpse can use corpses as an input resource, and that usage can remove the corpse when complete.
Core Rules.source: 2017 and 2023 forum discussions on corpse consumption

Internal Rules.text: Corpse targeting UI behavior is only active when a corpse-using skill is present in socketing context.
Internal Rules.source: forum thread on corpse highlighting/targeting behavior

Calculation Order.text: 1) locate candidate corpses 2) validate corpse availability to caller 3) apply consuming action 4) destroy or preserve corpse based on relevant preservation modifiers.
Calculation Order.source: community reports aligned to in-game behavior and support passives

Priority Rules.text: If a specific mechanic says a corpse is not destroyed (for example a preserved consume mechanic), that exception applies over default consume behavior.

Preconditions.text: One or more valid corpses must be present and in a state the consuming system accepts.
Preconditions.source: gameplay behavior in official forum examples

Postconditions.text: Consumed corpses stop being available to other consume or consume-dependent actions unless preserved by an explicit modifier.

Limitations.text: Not all deaths create meaningful usable corpses for every context, and corpse availability is constrained by engine/UI context.
Limitations.source: forum reports on corpse availability and interaction scope

Caps.text: No universal numeric cap on Corpse itself; caps are determined by individual consume or preservation mechanics.

Formula.text: No standalone formula for Corpse. Consume outcome follows per-mechanic rules: consume, preserve, or fail to consume.

prevents: consumption when not accepted by source context

text: Corpses generated by kill events or explicit generation systems
Dependencies.source: official patch notes and gameplay discussions

text: Corpse targeting expectations when no corpse-dependent skill is socketed
ambiguous consume outcomes from UI expectations
Itemized corpses and preserved corpses can follow special handling separate from normal corpse consume flow.
Exceptions.source: 3.24.0b patch note references for itemize/update behavior

Hidden Rules.text: Corpse-dependent pipelines are validated per source action; presence of a corpse target hotkey does not imply global eligibility.

Developer Notes.text: Model Corpse as an entity with state transitions (present, consumed, preserved, itemized) so patch-scope exceptions can be attached without merging unrelated behavior.

Historical Changes.text: Itemization and corpse UI interactions changed in recent platforms/patches; no single global rule rewrite identified for current batch.

text.note: Itemised corpses can be collected as a dedicated control option; corpse collect controls adjusted and itemized corpse workflow changed.
Corpses were part of map and combat contexts affected by map/system behavior updates.

text: Detonate Dead and similar skills consume corpses during use and typically make them unavailable for other consume uses.
Offering and summon-related corpse effects act through a consume path tied to corpse presence and source validity.
Corpse targeting key always works even without a corpse-using skill; it does not by default in some contexts.
All corpse appearances are equally usable for all systems.

prevents: use of consumed corpse by other actions
